name: config
context: Configure pnpm version pinning, workspace settings, catalogs, runtime policy, and package-manager behavior
info: This node covers pnpm configuration surfaces that change agent behavior before any command runs: `packageManager` pinning through Corepack, `pnpm-workspace.yaml` settings, runtime selection through `useNodeVersion` and `devEngines.runtime`, workspace catalogs, and strictness controls such as `packageManagerStrict` and `managePackageManagerVersions`. It matters because pnpm configuration determines what version of pnpm and Node are used, how dependencies are linked, whether catalogs are enforced, and how workspace-wide commands behave.
body:
# pnpm Configuration

pnpm spreads configuration across `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, and registry settings that are compatible with `.npmrc`. An agent should load this node before changing install behavior, workspace linking, or runtime pinning.

## Version pinning

The installation docs recommend using Corepack to pin pnpm in `package.json`:

```json
{
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.23.0"
}
```

With Corepack, `corepack use pnpm@latest-10` updates that field for the project. pnpm also checks `engines.pnpm` locally and can warn or fail depending on `engineStrict` and related settings.

## Workspace settings

`pnpm-workspace.yaml` controls package globs, catalogs, lockfile behavior, script execution, runtime pinning, and many install-time rules. Registry and auth details still live in the npm-compatible config surface documented in [[node/npm#npmrc]].

sections.title: Pin pnpm and Node versions
sections.body: Use the `packageManager` field to pin the pnpm CLI version when working through Corepack. In `package.json`, pnpm also supports `engines.pnpm` and `engines.runtime` or `devEngines.runtime` so the runtime version is resolved and stored in the lockfile. In `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `useNodeVersion` and `nodeVersion` can force a workspace-wide Node version policy.

sections.title: Workspace-wide settings
sections.body: Important workspace settings include `packageManagerStrict`, `managePackageManagerVersions`, `recursiveInstall`, `scriptShell`, `shellEmulator`, `requiredScripts`, and linker-related options such as `nodeLinker`, `hoist`, and `publicHoistPattern`. These settings change whether commands are allowed to run, which shell executes scripts, and how dependencies appear on disk. Load [[pnpm/store]] if a task depends on the exact node_modules layout.

sections.title: Catalogs, overrides, and workspace metadata
sections.body: Catalogs are reusable version constants defined in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and referenced with `catalog:` or `catalog:name`. `catalogMode` controls whether `pnpm add` must prefer or strictly obey those entries, and `cleanupUnusedCatalogs` removes dead catalog entries during install. In package manifests, `dependenciesMeta.*.injected` changes whether a workspace dependency is symlinked from source or copied into the virtual store to satisfy differing peer environments. Use catalogs for consistent versions across a monorepo; use metadata only when the peer model requires it.

sections.title: Runtime management
sections.body: pnpm can manage runtime versions directly. `devEngines.runtime` in `package.json` resolves a runtime version during install, stores the exact version in the lockfile, and makes scripts use that local runtime. `useNodeVersion` in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` pins the workspace runtime, while `executionEnv.nodeVersion` can pin per-project execution. This is useful when an agent must run scripts with the repo's intended Node version instead of the machine default.
